behaviors.description: Required patient fields (name, date of birth, insurance ID) are validated before any downstream processing and missing fields produce a clear error listing all missing items.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is validator-to-orchestrator handoff: test must prove downstream modules are not invoked when fields are missing, not just that a validator function can list errors.
behaviors.test_description: Run intake orchestrator with missing `name` and `insurance_id`; assert returned error lists both missing fields and insurance/triage/scheduling/confirmation adapters are not called.

behaviors.description: Date of birth validation rejects future dates, dates more than 150 years ago, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: Pure input-validation logic in one boundary; no module interaction is required to expose real failures.
behaviors.test_description: Call DOB validator directly with a future date, a >150-year-old date, and malformed strings; assert each is rejected with correct reason.

behaviors.description: The insurance eligibility check sends the correct patient ID and plan code to the payer API and correctly interprets `eligible` vs `not eligible` vs `pending`.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is an HTTP contract boundary. Real request serialization/transport and response handling can fail while mocks pass. Rejected Integration because behavior explicitly requires API payload correctness to payer API.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real HTTP test server endpoint; execute eligibility call and assert received request contains correct patient ID/plan code and returned statuses map to internal `eligible`/`not eligible`/`pending` states.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Higher level Agentic rejected: no AI/ML output quality is involved.

behaviors.description: When the payer API is unreachable, the system marks eligibility as `pending` and continues the intake rather than blocking.
behaviors.justification: Unreachable API is a real network/HTTP failure mode; mocked exceptions can miss actual timeout/connection-path behavior and continuation semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Point payer client to an unreachable host or blackholed test endpoint; run intake and assert eligibility becomes `pending` and pipeline proceeds to later steps.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Agentic/Workflow rejected: no model decision quality in scope.

behaviors.description: Triage invocation includes the reported symptoms and configured emergency rubric in the model input payload.
behaviors.justification: Plumbing-only payload construction and module handoff to model client; does not judge model output content, so Agentic is disqualified by scope.
behaviors.test_description: Run triage service with stub model adapter and capture outbound request; assert payload includes exact symptoms and configured emergency rubric fields.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System rejected because in-process stub catches payload-shape/routing bug; Agentic rejected by strict plumbing-only exception.

behaviors.description: The live AI symptom triage assigns high urgency to symptom combinations that indicate emergency conditions (for example chest pain plus shortness of breath).
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model clinical judgment; deterministic stubs would always hide this failure class.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live triage calls on emergency symptom sets (e.g., chest pain + shortness of breath) and assert urgency is high across repeated trials within acceptance threshold.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Workflow rejected: single model decision, not multi-agentic chain.

behaviors.description: The triage result parser stores a clinician-readable explanation field (not only a numeric score).
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at parser-to-persistence boundary: explanation may parse but be dropped/mis-mapped when stored.
behaviors.test_description: Feed deterministic triage response containing score + explanation through parser and repository; assert persisted record includes non-empty explanation field.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Agentic rejected because behavior tests storage/parsing presence, not explanation quality.

behaviors.description: The live AI symptom triage explanation cites case-specific symptoms and urgency rationale, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: This is output-quality evaluation of live AI explanation specificity/rationale; stubs cannot validate it.
behaviors.test_description: Run live triage on diverse symptom cases; assert explanation references case symptoms and includes urgency reasoning, failing generic/template-only text.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Workflow rejected: single agentic inference step.

behaviors.description: An appointment is scheduled in the first available slot that matches the assigned urgency level: high urgency gets same-day, medium gets within 48 hours.
behaviors.justification: Needs scheduler + calendar DB interaction to catch real slot-selection/state bugs; pure unit logic is insufficient for real availability data handling.
behaviors.test_description: Seed local calendar DB with candidate slots; run scheduler for high and medium urgency and assert selected slot is first valid one under same-day/48h constraints.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System rejected: local DB wiring is sufficient; no real OS/network dependency required.

behaviors.description: The scheduling step does not double-book a time slot even when two patients are processed concurrently.
behaviors.justification: Concurrency/transaction failure emerges in scheduler + shared DB interaction under parallel requests; this is beyond single-function unit scope.
behaviors.test_description: Run two concurrent scheduling attempts targeting same slot against local transactional DB; assert only one booking succeeds and other is retried/rejected without duplicate row.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System rejected: can be caught with in-process concurrency + local DB transaction semantics.

behaviors.description: The confirmation message includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and preparation instructions appropriate to visit type.
behaviors.justification: Template/rendering and field-selection logic can be validated within one function/object boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Call confirmation renderer with fixture appointment/patient/provider/visit-type data; assert output contains required fields and correct preparation instructions mapping.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration rejected: no required cross-module failure boundary in behavior text.

behaviors.description: Pipeline orchestration (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes correctly with deterministic stubbed triage output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets orchestration with deterministic stubbed triage; validates module sequencing/state handoff, not model quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run full intake pipeline in-process with stub triage output and stub payer responses; assert steps execute in order and final confirmation is produced with expected state.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Agentic rejected by explicit 'deterministic stubbed triage output' text; Workflow rejected because no real agentic steps.

behaviors.description: The full live intake pipeline (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes for a real patient submission without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Pipeline includes live AI triage; real model output can alter downstream path and success, so mocks hide real failures. This is one agentic stage, not a multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Submit real intake payload through end-to-end environment using live payer API path and live triage model; assert pipeline completes automatically and emits persisted appointment + confirmation.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Workflow rejected: only one real agentic decision stage is described.
